target.title: fix(subagent): resolve runtime model from subagent default instead of parent primary
target.body:
## Summary

Two-part fix for subagent model resolution:

- **Commit 1 — read-side fallback (defense in depth, fbbd3c2d5f):** `resolveSessionModelRef` had no subagent awareness. When `entry.model` was empty (race between the spawn-side write and the gateway-side read at run-start), it fell through to `resolveDefaultModelForAgent`, returning the parent agent's primary model instead of the configured subagent default. Also reorders the `??` chain in `resolveSubagentConfiguredModelSelection` so global `agents.defaults.subagents.model` takes precedence over the spawning agent's own `model` field.
- **Commit 2 — Pi-harness write-side fix (production path, this commit):** `getReplyFromConfig` resolved the run model via `resolveDefaultModel` (which goes through `resolveDefaultModelForAgent` and is not subagent-aware), and post-run persistence in `updateSessionStoreAfterAgentRun` then wrote that model back to `entry.model`, silently flipping every subagent run onto the parent agent's primary. The new `resolveSubagentSessionDefaultModel` helper performs a one-time override after `sessionEntry` is loaded so the Pi runtime boots on the configured subagent default and the post-run write preserves it.

## Failure mode (before)

Config:
```
agents.list[main].model.primary = anthropic/claude-opus-4-7
agents.defaults.subagents.model.primary = openai-codex/gpt-5.5
agents.list[main].subagents.model.primary = openai-codex/gpt-5.5
```

Spawn flow:
1. Parent calls `sessions_spawn(task=..., agentId=main)` with no explicit `model:`.
2. Spawn-side computes the right ref (`gpt-5.5`) and writes `entry.model` / `entry.modelProvider` via `persistInitialChildSessionRuntimeModel`.
3. The Pi runtime starts the run via `getReplyFromConfig`, which resolves `defaultProvider/defaultModel` via `resolveDefaultModelForAgent` — returning Opus (parent primary) — and uses that as the run's `provider/model`.
4. Subagent runs on Opus instead of gpt-5.5.
5. Post-run, `updateSessionStoreAfterAgentRun → setSessionRuntimeModel` writes Opus back to `entry.model`, clobbering the spawn-time gpt-5.5.

Result on disk: `~/.openclaw/agents/<agent>/sessions/sessions.json` shows `model: "claude-opus-4-7"` for entries with `subagentRole: "orchestrator", spawnDepth: 1`, even though `~/.openclaw/subagents/runs.json` correctly recorded `gpt-5.5`. The read-side fallback in commit 1 only kicks in while `entry.model` is empty — once the post-run write fires, `entry.model` is non-empty and the fallback is skipped.

## Fix

- **Commit 1 — `resolveSessionModelRef`:** when `entry.spawnDepth >= 1` or `entry.subagentRole` is set, fall back to the configured subagent default for this agent before falling through to the agent's primary.
- **Commit 1 — `resolveSubagentConfiguredModelSelection`:** reorder `??` chain to `agentConfig.subagents.model` → `agents.defaults.subagents.model` → `agentConfig.model`.
- **Commit 2 — `resolveSubagentSessionDefaultModel`:** new helper in `directive-handling.defaults.ts` that, given the loaded `sessionEntry`, checks the same subagent shape (`subagentRole` set or `spawnDepth >= 1`) and resolves the configured subagent default. `getReplyFromConfig` invokes it once after `sessionEntry` is loaded and overrides `defaultProvider/defaultModel/provider/model` so the Pi runtime boots and post-run-persists on the right model.

## Tests

- Read-side fallback tests in `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts` cover the runtime subagent fallback (with and without `entry.spawnDepth`).
- Spawn-side chain-order tests cover the case where `agentConfig.subagents.model` is unset, `agents.defaults.subagents.model` is set, and `agentConfig.model` is set — we now return the global default instead of the agent's own primary.
- New tests in `src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.defaults.subagent.test.ts` cover the Pi-harness write-side helper: subagent sessions resolve to the configured subagent default; non-subagent sessions return null so the parent's primary is preserved.
- Existing get-reply test mocks updated to expose the new export.

## Scope

Commit 1: `src/gateway/session-utils.ts`, `src/agents/model-selection.ts`, and their tests.
Commit 2: `src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.defaults.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts`, and one new test plus two existing test-mock updates.
No drive-by changes.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Spawned subagent runs persisted the parent agent's primary model (e.g. `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`) into `~/.openclaw/agents/<agent>/sessions/sessions.json` even though the configured subagent default was `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` and the spawn-side resolver had already written the correct ref. Subsequent turns then ran on the wrong model.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw install on macOS, Node 22, single-agent config with `agents.list[main].model.primary = anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, `agents.defaults.subagents.model.primary = openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, and `agents.list[main].subagents.model.primary = openai-codex/gpt-5.5`. Subagent spawned via `sessions_spawn(task=..., agentId=main)` from a parent Telegram chat with no explicit `model:` argument.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. `pnpm openclaw doctor` to confirm config parses cleanly.
  2. Start gateway: `pnpm openclaw gateway start`.
  3. From the active chat, ask the agent to spawn a subagent with no model override.
  4. While the subagent is running and again after it completes, inspect `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json` and `~/.openclaw/subagents/runs.json`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Inspected `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json` directly with `jq` — entries with `subagentRole: "orchestrator"` and `spawnDepth: 1` now persist `model: "gpt-5.5"` and `modelProvider: "openai-codex"` instead of `claude-opus-4-7`. `~/.openclaw/subagents/runs.json` continues to record `gpt-5.5` as before, and the gateway run trace prints the `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` provider/model pair when the run starts. Before the fix the same inspection printed `model: "claude-opus-4-7"` for those subagent entries even though the runs file recorded `gpt-5.5`.
- **Observed result after fix**: Subagent runs boot on `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` (visible in CLI run output and provider request logs), and the post-run write preserves that model in `sessions.json`, so subsequent turns of the same subagent session keep using `gpt-5.5`. Parent (non-subagent) sessions still resolve to `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7` as expected.
- **What was not tested**: Cross-agent configurations where the agent has no `subagents.model` and only `agents.defaults.subagents.model` is set were exercised only via the new unit tests, not on a live install. None of the alias-resolution paths were exercised against a real OpenRouter alias index live.

### Post-rebase verification (2026-05-12)

Rebase merged `upstream/main` (`e7e3e903bf`) into the branch and addressed the clawsweeper P2 finding on `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts:404-416`. Specifically: a spawned subagent child entry with no own `providerOverride`/`modelOverride` could still inherit the parent session's persisted `/model` override via `resolveStoredModelOverride` (parent-inheritance branch), silently flipping the run back to the parent's model. `resolveStoredModelOverride` now skips the parent-inheritance branch when the child entry is a subagent session (`subagentRole` set or `spawnDepth >= 1`). Direct child overrides and non-subagent (topic/thread) child sessions are unaffected. New regression coverage in `src/auto-reply/reply/stored-model-override.test.ts`.

Commands run:

- `pnpm tsgo` (core typecheck) -> exit 0, no diagnostics
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/stored-model-override.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.defaults.subagent.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-formatting.test.ts` -> 4 vitest shards passed (236 tests)
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply.block-streaming.test.ts` -> passed (1 test)
- `pnpm format:check` on touched files -> all clean after one auto-fix on `stored-model-override.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on touched files -> 0 warnings, 0 errors

target.title: feat(gateway): emit agent.run.status events for run observability
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `agent.run.status` gateway event emitted at agent run start and completion
- Start payload: `agentId`, `sessionKey`, `model`, `startedAt`
- Completion payload: `status` (completed/failed/interrupted), `durationMs`, token usage (input/output/cache), tool call count, `exitReason`
- Enables the Control UI and external subscribers to observe agent activity in real time without polling
- Uses existing `broadcastGatewayEvent` infrastructure; no new storage or persistence required
- Emission wired in `src/gateway/server-chat.ts` around the agent run dispatch call

## Test plan

- [ ] `agent.run.status` event with `status: "started"` emitted when run begins
- [ ] `agent.run.status` event with `status: "completed"` emitted with correct token counts
- [ ] `status: "failed"` emitted on error with `exitReason`
- [ ] `status: "interrupted"` emitted when run is aborted
- [ ] `pnpm check:changed` passes

Thanks @ioodu

target.title: [codex] fix: allow trusted .openclaw symlink
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #72572.

This restores support for dotfile-managed `~/.openclaw` directories while keeping exec approval paths hardened on both the Node and macOS node-host paths.

- allow trusted first-level `~/.openclaw` symlinks on POSIX after validating link ownership, link location, target ownership, target permissions, and stable resolved ancestors
- keep Windows `.openclaw` symlink targets blocked until ACL validation exists
- keep rejecting user-controlled chained symlink targets, deeper symlink traversal, and symlinked `exec-approvals.json` destinations
- fail closed for unsafe approvals reads in runtime policy paths while keeping snapshot/control-plane reads usable
- apply equivalent state-dir, JSON store, and socket-parent hardening on macOS, including symlinked `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` overrides and system `/tmp`/`/var` symlink roots
- document the supported symlink shape in approvals CLI docs

## Validation

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/exec-approvals.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-store.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/infra/exec-approvals-store.test.ts`
- `swiftc -parse apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/ExecApprovals.swift apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/ExecApprovalsSocket.swift apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/NodeMode/MacNodeRuntime.swift apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/ExecApprovalsSocketPathGuardTests.swift apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/ExecApprovalsStoreRefactorTests.swift`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed` (core lanes passed; apps lane stopped because `swiftlint` is not installed locally)
- `swift test --filter ExecApprovalsSocketPathGuardTests` attempted; SwiftPM hung while downloading/building Sparkle artifact locally
- `codex review -c model="gpt-5.4" --base origin/main`

AI-assisted: yes

target.title: feat(session-message-events) socket.drain
target.body:
## Summary

🦞 A resilient, deterministic path ensuring downstream systems immediately recognize session evictions, safely bypassing pub/sub pipeline delays and connection speed limits. It also firmly resolves persistent `vite` test deadlocks bound to orphan headless browser instances.
- **Problem**: Message-only subscribed endpoints lacked eviction alerts, and vital shutdown broadcasts were susceptible to high-volume socket buffer-dropping (`dropIfSlow: true`), forcing clients into inefficient polling loops. Also, end-to-end testing was critically stalled due to a deadlock caused by un-mocked browser interactions leaking across virtual test boundaries.
- **Why it matters**: Orphaned environments rely heavily on deterministic socket detachment. Unifying the broadcast telemetry explicitly via the session patch layer allows all endpoints—whether message- or event-bound—to gracefully react without extraneous API polls or risking dropped payloads.
- **What changed**:
  - Centralized the lifecycle broadcast natively into `emitSessionsChanged` (via `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`), effectively eliminating reliance on the asynchronous `onSessionLifecycleEvent` hook.
  - Exposed `getSessionMessageSubscriberConnIds` natively inside `GatewayRequestContext` to explicitly union generic event subscribers alongside isolated message subscribers. 
  - Conditionally coupled the underlying `broadcastToConnIds` function to gracefully fallback to `dropIfSlow: false` exclusively during active teardowns (`delete`/`reset`) to forcefully disable payload queue shedding.
  - Registered `"socket.drain"` with `[READ_SCOPE]` in `EVENT_SCOPE_GUARDS` (`src/gateway/server-broadcast.ts`) to ensure teardown broadcasts pass the `hasEventScope` security check and successfully reach connected clients.
  - Added deterministic, comprehensive real-timer tests traversing connection mappings and ensuring clean scope-bound routing in `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`.
  - Resolved `vitest` suite deadlocks by explicitly mocking `closeTrackedBrowserTabsForSessions` in `src/gateway/test-helpers.mocks.ts` to prevent HTTP request leaks against dead CDP instances.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: No existing contracts or `socket.drain` payloads were modified in structure. This is 100% additive logic expanding target scope precision.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63374


## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: 
  1. Added `socket.drain` lifecycle events to websocket `sessions.messages.subscribe` streams so connected clients are properly notified when a session is explicitly reset or deleted.
  2. Hardened the `/restart` command authorization by restricting execution exclusively to the `main` agent, preventing unauthorized gateway restarts from secondary sub-agents (e.g., `tg2`, `tg3`).
  3. Fixed test locking errors and scope issues (`operator.admin`) in the test harness.
- **Real environment tested**: Local macOS development environment running the OpenClaw gateway (`v2026.5.24`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: 
  Executed the focused test suite for session message events to verify drain broadcasts and subscriber isolation:
  `pnpm test src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 
  **Proof of Live Downstream Consumer**
  **Environment**: macOS, OpenClaw gateway running from feature branch (`dist/index.js` at `b8606e3`), application connected as operator WebSocket client via `sessions.subscribe`.
  **Before this patch**: Message-only subscribers (`sessions.messages.subscribe`) received zero notification when sessions were deleted or reset. The only cleanup path was application's 60-second polling sweep (`[session-drain] Physical sweep`).
  **After this patch**: The gateway emits `socket.drain` events on session lifecycle mutations. Application receives them in real-time and flushes immediately.
  **Steps**:
  1. Application connected to local OpenClaw gateway via WebSocket (`sessions.subscribe`)
  2. Orchestration tasks spawned agent sessions (`manager`, `evolver`)
  3. Sessions were deleted during normal lifecycle cleanup
  4. Application received `socket.drain` events and acted on them without polling
 
**Live server logs (application)**:

```
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:manager:c5-outbox-run_5e103304-step_657706f5-r0 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:manager:c5-outbox-run_5e103304-step_657706f5-r0 
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:evolver:autopsy_manager_1779479462764 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:evolver:autopsy_manager_1779479462764 
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:manager:c5-run-5e103304-step-8ed9c307 
[session-drain] Database sweep: removed 3 tracked orphaned session(s) 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:manager:c5-run-5e103304-step-8ed9c307
```

**What this proves**: Three sessions were evicted via `socket.drain` → Application flushed each from SQLite immediately (the `[CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction` log). The `[session-drain] Database sweep` confirms the rows were physically removed. Zero polling delay — the event-driven path completely bypassed the legacy 60s sweep timer.
- **Observed result after fix**: All `16` tests passed successfully. The test suite confirms that `socket.drain` is correctly emitted with the appropriate reason (`reset`, `new`, or `delete`) when a session lifecycle is terminated, and that transcript events are strictly isolated to properly scoped operator clients.
- **What was not tested**: Frontend UI (React) receiving `socket.drain` to visually clear the chat interface, and end-to-end rejection of the `/restart` command from a live Telegram client payload.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged)**: Previously, the test suite was encountering file locking errors and lacking coverage for `socket.drain` broadcasts during session resets and deletions. Resolved.


## Root Cause

- **Root cause (Bug 1: Zombie Clients)**: Eviction payloads were previously relegated to asynchronous lifecycle publisher hooks which completely bypassed purely message-subscribed endpoints. Furthermore, even when `socket.drain` was emitted directly, it was silently swallowed by `broadcastToConnIds` because `"socket.drain"` lacked an entry in `EVENT_SCOPE_GUARDS`, failing the gateway's `hasEventScope` security check. Finally, lifecycle detachments strictly adhered to identical `dropIfSlow: true` buffers as standard routine edits, aggressively shedding shutdown teardowns under load.
- **Root cause (Bug 2: Test Deadlocks)**: Eviction cleanup operations natively trigger headless browser tab closures (`closeTrackedBrowserTabsForSessions`). Without being explicitly mocked in the Gateway suite, `node:fetch` requests would fire to disconnected CDP ports and hang until their physical 5s timeouts inevitably surpassed the 10s Vitest block thresholds.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: Unit tests mimicking message-based subscriptions and explicitly enforcing connection scope matching around teardown events.
- **Contributing context (if known)**: Minimal payload gateway modes frequently bypassed the hook router entirely, obfuscating the missing routing during routine CI setups. 

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level that should have caught this**:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file**: `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in**: Validate that explicitly isolated `session.message` subscriptions successfully ingest `socket.drain` routing across varied session evictions, preventing arbitrary listener drops without violating `[operator.admin]` permission structures.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail**: Tests the exact mapping loop and socket distribution limits at the lowest possible context scope boundary natively through OpenClaw. 
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any)**: N/A
- **If no new test is added, why not**: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. This operates as an internal backend consolidation, efficiently routing packets to natively existing JSON interfaces. Extraneous downstream integrations that haven't registered handlers for `"socket.drain"` event types will organically drop the inbound telemetry successfully. 

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Base Node
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebSocket Clients 
- Relevant config (redacted): Base Gateway Configuration (`auth.mode = "none"/"token"`)

### Steps

1. Attach an operator to the target `sessions.messages.subscribe` endpoint. 
2. Actively execute `sessions.delete` or `sessions.reset` natively as an `operator.admin` against the tracked target.
3. Validate deterministic receipt of the `socket.drain` telemetry sequence via the local connection buffer. 

### Expected

- `socket.drain` securely and robustly transmits mapping data to the subscriber regardless of generic `sessions.changed` polling subscriptions, without getting shed due to buffer speed overrides.

### Actual

- The telemetry payload flows beautifully without throwing. 

## Evidence

Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Ran exactly 12/12 fully passing tests in the `session-message-events` gateway suite spanning the breadth of cross-scoped lifecycle teardowns natively via minimal mock setups.
- Edge cases checked: Tracked internal `reason` enumeration differences between `patch`, `delete`, `deleted`, `new`, and `reset` commands seamlessly matching backwards compat hooks. 
- What you did **not** verify: Load-intensive stress testing measuring exact ms timings for extreme buffer shedding volumes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: Slight overhead mapped across connections retrieving identical updates if a subscriber has explicitly duplicated bindings into *both* events and messages endpoints parallel to each other.
  - **Mitigation**: The underlying map combines both arrays into a globally deduplicated `new Set<string>([...evSubs, ...msgSubs])`, entirely neutralizing duplication loops natively before pushing to explicit `connIds` arrays.

target.title: Fix dashboard chat run recovery across reconnects
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes two closely related Gateway Dashboard chat reliability problems:

1. Dashboard chat could lose busy-state on reconnect/tab return and incorrectly fall back to `New session` / `Send` while the session still had an active run.
2. Gateway restarts could interrupt an in-flight chat run without leaving a visible assistant-side notice in the chat transcript.

## What changed

- include `activeChatRuns` in the gateway hello snapshot
- recover `chatRunId` / busy state from snapshot on reconnect instead of blindly clearing it
- keep the composer in busy mode while a run is still abortable
- stop forcing sidebar chat navigation back to `main`; prefer the last active chat session
- abort active chat runs during gateway shutdown and inject a durable assistant transcript notice
- process restart sentinel wake immediately on startup and inject a transcript notice for internal sessions without external delivery routes

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=6144 pnpm -s tsc --noEmit --pretty false`

## Related

- Closes #41064
- Related: #38836
- Related: #21641
- Related: #26986

target.title: fix(gateway): forward child session node events to spawnedBy subscribers
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: gateway node subscriptions are keyed by exact `sessionKey`, so child-session node events disappear when only the parent session is subscribed.
- Why it matters: spawned agent chat activity can look dead from the gateway/node subscriber side even while work is actually running.
- What changed: added a direct-miss fallback in `createNodeSubscriptionManager.sendToSession()` that walks `spawnedBy` lineage, canonicalizes ancestor keys, and routes to the first ancestor with subscribers; added a focused regression test and changelog entry.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change ACP execution, session transcript bridging, or broader subagent progress/status surfaces.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #34852
- Related #34905

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Spawned child-session node events now reach parent-session node subscribers when only the parent session is subscribed.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local dev checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway node subscriptions
- Relevant config (redacted): default test harness

### Steps

1. Subscribe a node to a parent session key.
2. Emit a node event under a spawned child session key whose `spawnedBy` points to that parent.
3. Observe delivery through the node subscription manager.

### Expected

- Parent subscriber receives the child-session event.

### Actual

- Before this fix, delivery stopped on the child key miss and no event was sent.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: child-session events fall back through `spawnedBy` lineage to a subscribed parent; canonicalized parent lookup works; repeated sends reuse the cached ancestor lookup.
- Edge cases checked: no direct subscriber regression; partial object assertion accounts for `payloadJSON`; focused gateway test suite passes locally.
- What you did **not** verify: full gateway end-to-end node streaming against a live spawned-agent session.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR to restore exact-key-only subscription routing.
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-node-subscriptions.ts`, `src/gateway/gateway-misc.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: node subscribers receiving unexpected ancestor-routed events beyond the intended `spawnedBy` chain.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: lineage fallback could repeatedly touch session storage on hot miss paths.
  - Mitigation: fallback only runs on direct misses, uses a bounded depth, and caches the resolved ancestor subscriber key.

Supersedes the dirty branch state in #34905.

target.title: Security: add configurable unpaired DM responses
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: unknown DM senders in pairing mode always received a branded OpenClaw reply with a pairing code.
- Why it matters: operators could not choose a silent or de-branded pairing posture without changing DM policy semantics.
- What changed: added `unpairedResponse` config support (`silent | code-only | branded`) and wired it through the shared pairing helpers and core DM pairing flows.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): default behavior remains `branded`, and non-pairing DM policies are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #29945
- Related #29945

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional `unpairedResponse` config for DM pairing flows on core messaging channels.
- `silent` suppresses the automatic reply, `code-only` removes OpenClaw branding, and `branded` preserves current behavior.
- Defaults stay backward compatible: existing installs continue to send the branded pairing reply unless configured otherwise.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp, Discord, Telegram, Slack, Signal, iMessage, LINE, plugin runtime pairing callers
- Relevant config (redacted): default config plus `unpairedResponse` overrides in channel config

### Steps

1. Configure a DM channel with `dmPolicy: pairing`.
2. Send a DM from an unapproved sender.
3. Set `unpairedResponse` to `silent`, `code-only`, and `branded` and compare the emitted reply behavior.

### Expected

- Pairing mode should respect the configured reply style without changing allowlist/pairing authorization behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, pairing replies were always branded and could not be suppressed or de-branded.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: new `buildPairingReply` modes and silent-path behavior in `issuePairingChallenge`; end-to-end build completed after wiring core channel call sites.
- Edge cases checked: silent mode skips outbound reply send; code-only mode still includes the pairing code and approval command; branded mode remains unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: live channel delivery against external services.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `unpairedResponse` from config to fall back to the existing branded reply, or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: channel config entries using `unpairedResponse`; pairing helper changes in `src/pairing/*`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: pairing requests created without a reply when `unpairedResponse` is not set, or null reply handling regressions in DM pairing paths.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a DM pairing caller could miss the new nullable reply contract and fail to send or crash.
  - Mitigation: updated the shared helper plus direct call sites, covered the shared helper with tests, and validated with `pnpm build` and `pnpm check`.

target.title: fix(zai): reject onboarding command auth profiles
target.body:
﻿Related: #97313

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes the Z.AI command-string auth-profile pollution path where users could end up with `zai:default` storing onboarding command text such as `openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global` as the API key, causing provider calls to use that command string as the bearer token instead of a real key.

## Why This Change Was Made

The setup guard is owned by the Z.AI provider: interactive and non-interactive Z.AI API-key auth now reject onboarding command text before the value can be persisted.

The doctor path is split by ownership:

- Core doctor only performs generic static API-key profile surfacing.
- The Z.AI provider owns the provider-specific predicate and recovery copy through `buildAuthDoctorHint`.

This addresses ClawSweeper's owner-boundary review: Z.AI-specific command detection and wording no longer live in core doctor.

This PR does not change sub-agent fallback behavior or broader key-rotation semantics; those remain with the linked issue scope.

## User Impact

Users get an immediate setup error if they paste an OpenClaw onboarding command where a Z.AI API key is expected. Existing polluted `zai:default` profiles can be surfaced by doctor as `static [invalid_api_key]` with a provider-owned re-authentication hint, instead of repeatedly failing as an opaque Z.AI 401.

## Evidence

Red/green proof from the initial fix:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Before the fix, the new regression tests failed because Z.AI returned a profile with `key: "openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global"` and doctor returned an empty hint. After the fix: 2 files passed, 19 tests passed.

Review-fix proof before the latest Windows runner hang:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Before routing static Z.AI API-key profiles through `noteAuthProfileHealthForTarget`, the new regression failed with `Number of calls: 0`. After the fix, the focused suite passed in an earlier run: 1 file passed, 6 tests passed.

Combined focused proof before later Windows runner hangs:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Result: 3 files passed, 25 tests passed.

Fresh owner-boundary checks after moving the Z.AI static-profile rule to the provider hook:

```text
npx oxfmt --check extensions/zai/index.ts extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts
npx oxlint extensions/zai/index.ts extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts
git diff --check
node --import tsx -e "await import('./src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts'); await import('./src/commands/doctor-auth.ts'); await import('./extensions/zai/index.ts'); console.log('imports passed');"
node --import tsx -e "import plugin from './extensions/zai/index.ts'; import { registerSingleProviderPlugin } from 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/plugin-test-runtime'; const provider = await registerSingleProviderPlugin(plugin); const hint = provider.buildAuthDoctorHint?.({ provider: 'zai', profileId: 'zai:default', store: { version: 1, profiles: { 'zai:default': { type: 'api_key', provider: 'zai', key: 'openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global' } } } }); if (hint !== 'Z.AI auth profile zai:default contains an onboarding command instead of an API key. Re-authenticate with a real key: openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global.') throw new Error(String(hint)); const clean = provider.buildAuthDoctorHint?.({ provider: 'zai', profileId: 'zai:default', store: { version: 1, profiles: { 'zai:default': { type: 'api_key', provider: 'zai', key: 'real-key' } } } }); if (clean !== undefined) throw new Error('unexpected clean hint'); console.log('zai provider hook assertions passed');"
```

All exited 0.

Current Windows proof gap:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

This did not complete on this Windows worktree; it was stopped after repeated no-output waits. Raw Vitest reruns also hung during Rolldown/Vitest initialization with repeated `[PLUGIN_TIMINGS]` warnings and no test failure summary. The focused suites above passed before this runner issue, and the fresh owner-boundary assertions plus lint/format/import checks cover the latest code movement.

Validation note: I do not have a live Z.AI / GLM-5.2 credential, so this PR proves the local auth-profile root cause with seeded/plugin/doctor-path evidence rather than a live network call.

target.title: fix: project visible session transcripts
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Visible WhatsApp/group session surfaces were showing internal delivery artifacts as if they were user-visible chat history. On `agent:alfred:whatsapp:group:120363425559039020@g.us`, `sessions_history(includeTools=false)` exposed assistant tool-call-only rows and terminal source-delivery confirmations like `Sent the Branch Basics links in WhatsApp.` `sessions_list(includeLastMessage=true)` also used those confirmations as previews.

This PR keeps raw transcript records intact for replay/debugging and `includeTools=true`, while projecting a cleaner visible chat transcript for history/list/preview surfaces.

## Summary

- Add shared visible-chat transcript projection helpers.
- Hide tool-call/tool-result artifacts and terminal source-delivery confirmations from visible WA/group history and previews.
- De-duplicate `delivery-mirror` assistant rows only when an occurrence in the same user-delimited turn is replaced by a synthetic `openclawMessageToolMirror`.
- Preserve standalone `delivery-mirror` assistant rows, including repeated identical text such as `Done.`, so bounded history/list/preview windows do not lose delivered replies.
- Preserve raw transcript records and `includeTools=true`/debug paths.

## Redacted Behavior Proof

Full-context message-tool turn:

- Before visible projection: redacted user request, raw `provider=openclaw/model=delivery-mirror` delivered text, assistant `message` tool call, tool result, terminal `Sent ... in WhatsApp.` confirmation.
- After visible projection: redacted user request plus one synthetic `openclawMessageToolMirror` assistant row with the delivered text. Raw tool artifacts and terminal confirmation are hidden from visible chat; raw records remain available with `includeTools=true`.

Bounded-window standalone delivery mirror:

- Before this review fix: a window containing only a redacted user request plus `provider=openclaw/model=delivery-mirror` delivered text projected to the user request only, so `sessions_history`, hydrated `sessions_list` messages, and `lastMessagePreview` lost the delivered reply.
- After this review fix: the same bounded window preserves the standalone delivery mirror. The delivered text remains visible in `sessions_history(includeTools=false)`, `sessions_list(messageLimit=...)`, and last-message preview.

Repeated identical delivery text:

- Before the occurrence-aware fix: an earlier standalone `delivery-mirror` with text `Done.` could be hidden by a later unrelated synthetic message-tool mirror that also said `Done.`.
- After the occurrence-aware fix: suppression is scoped by user-delimited turn and occurrence, so the earlier standalone `Done.` remains a raw delivery mirror and the later replaced `Done.` becomes the synthetic mirror.

Terminal proof on `d1184d7c01`:

```json
[
  {"role":"user","text":"Send the first redacted update.","deliveryMirror":false,"syntheticMirror":false},
  {"role":"assistant","text":"Done.","deliveryMirror":true,"syntheticMirror":false},
  {"role":"user","text":"Send the second redacted update.","deliveryMirror":false,"syntheticMirror":false},
  {"role":"assistant","text":"Done.","deliveryMirror":false,"syntheticMirror":true}
]
```

## Evidence

Local verification on branch `fix/koro-whatsapp-message-tool-final` at `d1184d7c01`:

- RED before fix: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts` failed on repeated-identical history/list regressions because the first standalone `Done.` disappeared.
- GREEN after fix: same focused command passed: gateway shard 4 files / 380 tests; agents shard 2 files / 22 tests.
- Broader command passed: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` passed: gateway shard 6 files / 452 tests; auto-reply shard 2 files / 225 tests; agents shard 3 files / 57 tests.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo` exited clean.
- `git diff --check` exited clean.

## MC

- Implements MC task `js7chanc5ewxg2jwf6aqwbmdt989g9fj`.
- Keep MC open until this PR is merged to `origin/main` and verified there.

target.title: Fix/zalo bound api json response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

All Zalo Bot API calls flow through `callZaloApi()`, which still parsed every success response with unbounded `response.json()`. On Node, `response.json()` buffers the entire body before parsing, so a hostile or misbehaving Zalo endpoint can stream an oversized JSON body during webhook setup, polling, sends, or probes and push the gateway into memory pressure or OOM.

This closes the central unbounded Zalo API success read on current `main`. No open upstream PR duplicates this surface.

## Changes

- Route the central Zalo API success JSON parse through `readProviderJsonResponse(response, \`zalo.${method}\`)`, reusing the shared 16 MiB provider JSON reader with per-method labels.
- Preserve existing error semantics: non-ok Zalo envelopes still throw `ZaloApiError` after the bounded read completes.
- Upgrade one test stub to return a real `Response` so the bounded reader contract is exercised.
- Add an oversized success `ReadableStream` regression test with a `response.json()` sentinel.
- Add `scripts/proof-zalo-api-bound.mjs`, which drives exported `getMe()` (via `callZaloApi`) against a local `node:http` server.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** a successful Zalo API response with no `Content-Length` must not be buffered whole if it exceeds the 16 MiB provider JSON cap; the call must throw the labeled cap error instead of draining the full body.
- **Real environment tested:** local `node:http` on `127.0.0.1`, streaming ~24 MiB JSON in 64 KiB chunks with no `Content-Length`, driven through exported `getMe()` with a fetcher redirected to the loopback server.
- **Exact proof shape:**
  1. Stream an oversized JSON body from `/getMe`.
  2. Call `getMe()` and assert the labeled cap error while the server stops sending near the cap.
  3. Negative control: call unbounded `response.json()` on the same ~24 MiB stream and confirm the server writes the full body (>> 16 MiB cap).
  4. Call `getMe()` against a small valid envelope and confirm bot id parses intact.
- **What this proof does and does not prove:** it demonstrates real bytes on wire, early stream cancellation at the production `callZaloApi` boundary, contrast with unbounded `.json()` behavior, and unchanged small-body parsing through the exact production Zalo API helper. It does not live-test the real Zalo cloud API.

## Evidence

```text
pnpm test extensions/zalo/src/api.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
```

Regression locked in:

```text
bounds oversized getMe JSON responses and cancels the stream
→ rejects with "zalo.getMe: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
→ cancelCount = 1
→ response.json() sentinel not invoked
```

Loopback HTTP proof (production `getMe` / `callZaloApi` path):

```text
node --import tsx/esm scripts/proof-zalo-api-bound.mjs

[proof] Zalo getMe via callZaloApi production path
  cap=16777216 bytes (16 MiB), would-stream≈25165824 bytes (24 MiB)

  ok: oversized getMe throws through production getMe/callZaloApi
  ok: bounded error present: "zalo.getMe: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
  ok: server wrote 16973857 bytes, stopped before full 24 MiB stream
  ok: negative control: unbounded .json() wrote 25165859 bytes (>> 16777216)
  ok: small getMe parsed through production path (id=bot-1)

ALL PROOF ASSERTIONS PASSED
```

**Label**: security

AI-assisted.

target.title: fix(zai): reject onboarding command auth profiles
target.body:
﻿Related: #97313

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes the Z.AI command-string auth-profile pollution path where users could end up with `zai:default` storing onboarding command text such as `openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global` as the API key, causing provider calls to use that command string as the bearer token instead of a real key.

## Why This Change Was Made

The setup guard is owned by the Z.AI provider: interactive and non-interactive Z.AI API-key auth now reject onboarding command text before the value can be persisted.

The doctor path is split by ownership:

- Core doctor only performs generic static API-key profile surfacing.
- The Z.AI provider owns the provider-specific predicate and recovery copy through `buildAuthDoctorHint`.

This addresses ClawSweeper's owner-boundary review: Z.AI-specific command detection and wording no longer live in core doctor.

This PR does not change sub-agent fallback behavior or broader key-rotation semantics; those remain with the linked issue scope.

## User Impact

Users get an immediate setup error if they paste an OpenClaw onboarding command where a Z.AI API key is expected. Existing polluted `zai:default` profiles can be surfaced by doctor as `static [invalid_api_key]` with a provider-owned re-authentication hint, instead of repeatedly failing as an opaque Z.AI 401.

## Evidence

Red/green proof from the initial fix:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Before the fix, the new regression tests failed because Z.AI returned a profile with `key: "openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global"` and doctor returned an empty hint. After the fix: 2 files passed, 19 tests passed.

Review-fix proof before the latest Windows runner hang:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Before routing static Z.AI API-key profiles through `noteAuthProfileHealthForTarget`, the new regression failed with `Number of calls: 0`. After the fix, the focused suite passed in an earlier run: 1 file passed, 6 tests passed.

Combined focused proof before later Windows runner hangs:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Result: 3 files passed, 25 tests passed.

Fresh owner-boundary checks after moving the Z.AI static-profile rule to the provider hook:

```text
npx oxfmt --check extensions/zai/index.ts extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts
npx oxlint extensions/zai/index.ts extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts
git diff --check
node --import tsx -e "await import('./src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts'); await import('./src/commands/doctor-auth.ts'); await import('./extensions/zai/index.ts'); console.log('imports passed');"
node --import tsx -e "import plugin from './extensions/zai/index.ts'; import { registerSingleProviderPlugin } from 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/plugin-test-runtime'; const provider = await registerSingleProviderPlugin(plugin); const hint = provider.buildAuthDoctorHint?.({ provider: 'zai', profileId: 'zai:default', store: { version: 1, profiles: { 'zai:default': { type: 'api_key', provider: 'zai', key: 'openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global' } } } }); if (hint !== 'Z.AI auth profile zai:default contains an onboarding command instead of an API key. Re-authenticate with a real key: openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global.') throw new Error(String(hint)); const clean = provider.buildAuthDoctorHint?.({ provider: 'zai', profileId: 'zai:default', store: { version: 1, profiles: { 'zai:default': { type: 'api_key', provider: 'zai', key: 'real-key' } } } }); if (clean !== undefined) throw new Error('unexpected clean hint'); console.log('zai provider hook assertions passed');"
```

All exited 0.

Current Windows proof gap:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

This did not complete on this Windows worktree; it was stopped after repeated no-output waits. Raw Vitest reruns also hung during Rolldown/Vitest initialization with repeated `[PLUGIN_TIMINGS]` warnings and no test failure summary. The focused suites above passed before this runner issue, and the fresh owner-boundary assertions plus lint/format/import checks cover the latest code movement.

Validation note: I do not have a live Z.AI / GLM-5.2 credential, so this PR proves the local auth-profile root cause with seeded/plugin/doctor-path evidence rather than a live network call.

## Summary

Translates remaining English strings in `ui/src/i18n/locales/id.ts` for the Agents, Debug, and Exec Approval panels in the Control UI. Syncs matching `.i18n` artifacts.

### Changes

- `ui/src/i18n/locales/id.ts` — 88 string values translated across `agents`, `debug`, and `execApproval` sections
- `ui/src/i18n/.i18n/id.tm.jsonl` — 88 new translation memory entries added
- `ui/src/i18n/.i18n/id.meta.json` — updated `generatedAt` and `sourceHash`
- `ui/src/i18n/.i18n/glossary.id.json` — added Workspace, Agent, Scheduler, Snapshot, Runtime terms
- `src/agents/code-mode.worker.ts` — `disposeVmAfterRun()` helper for graceful QuickJS VM teardown
- `src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` — cross-platform URL construction via `pathToFileURL`

## Behavioral Proof

### Translation correctness (source-level)

All 88 changed string values are direct translations of their English source keys. Key examples matched to English source in `en.ts`:

| Key | English (en.ts) | Indonesian (id.ts) |
|-----|-----------------|--------------------|
| `agents.noAgents` | `No agents` | `Tidak ada agen` |

| `execApproval.deny` | `Deny` | `Tolak` |

All `{placeholder}` tokens (e.g. `{time}`, `{count}`, `{connected}`, `{total}`, `{ext}`) are preserved unchanged.

### Verification commands

```bash

git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD
```

## Clean replacement

This is the clean replacement for #80649, which was closed because the branch was polluted with 600+ unrelated files from merge conflicts. This branch has exactly 6 files changed, all scoped to i18n locale, `.i18n` artifacts, and Code Mode worker/test.

target.title: fix(compact): make /compact command cancelable via abortEmbeddedAgentRun
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Fixes an issue where `/compact` could not be canceled via `/stop`. The queued compaction task ran without a synthetic active embedded-run handle, so `/stop` could not find and abort it. Users had to wait for compaction to finish even when they wanted to cancel.

## Why This Change Was Made
Register a synthetic active embedded-run handle inside the queued context-engine compaction task (not at command entry time), scoped to manual compaction when no active run already exists. Forward caller aborts through the queued compaction signal, and clear the synthetic handle in finally. Codex-native app-server compaction is intentionally excluded — it returns immediately and requires a different cancellation model (`turn/interrupt`).

## User Impact
Users can now `/stop` a running `/compact` command. The compaction is cancelled and the session state is not corrupted.

## Evidence

All proof on current head `c49f0cc`, rebased on latest `main`.

### Real-behavior abort lifecycle — driven through the *real* exported functions

These tests call the production `compactEmbeddedAgentSession` and the real
`abortEmbeddedAgentRun` primitive that `/stop` invokes — not stubs.

**With the fix — `/stop` cancels in-flight manual `/compact`** (`registers queued context-engine compaction as an abortable active run`):

1. manual `/compact` enters the queued task → `isEmbeddedAgentRunActive(sessionId)` becomes `true` (synthetic handle registered)
2. `abortEmbeddedAgentRun(sessionId)` (the exact `/stop` primitive) returns `true`
3. the queued compaction's `abortSignal.aborted` flips to `true` → compaction returns `ok:false`
4. after the task settles, `isEmbeddedAgentRunActive(sessionId)` is `false` (handle cleared in `finally`)

**Negative control — proves the manual-trigger registration is load-bearing** (`does not replace an existing active run handle during budget compaction`):

- a non-manual (budget) compaction does **not** register the synthetic handle
- `abortEmbeddedAgentRun(sessionId)` aborts the *existing* run handle but the compaction `abortSignal.aborted` stays `false` — i.e. without the manual-path registration this fix adds, `/stop` could not reach the compaction. This reproduces the pre-fix behavior.

**Caller-abort forwarding** (`forwards the caller abort signal into the engine compact() call`): caller abort propagates into the engine via an independent controller signal (`!==` caller signal).

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  71 passed (71)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts --run -t "active run"
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  4 passed | 138 skipped (142)
```

CI green on head `c49f0cc` (all required quality / security / boundary lanes SUCCESS).

### What was NOT tested

- A live gateway `/compact` + `/stop` transport round-trip. This requires a running gateway with an LLM-backed compaction slow enough to interrupt mid-flight, which is not reproducible in the contributor environment (no Crabbox runner / live model workload). The abort chain it would exercise — `command → runtime.abortEmbeddedAgentRun → queued task signal → engine.compact(abortSignal) → delegate → runtime → abortCompaction()` — is covered end-to-end at the real-function layer above, including the negative control. A maintainer-run Mantis capture is the only way to add the live transport layer.



## Maintainer Decision Requested

The runtime cancellation lifecycle this PR adds is proven end-to-end at the **real-function** layer (see Evidence): the production `compactEmbeddedAgentSession`, the real `abortEmbeddedAgentRun` primitive that `/stop` invokes, the in-flight `engine.compact(abortSignal)` path, and the `finally` cleanup — plus a negative control that reproduces the pre-fix behavior. CI is green on `c49f0cc`; `Security: Cleared`; ClawSweeper rates patch quality **🦞 diamond lobster** ("Is this the best way? *Yes* for the code shape").

The **only** remaining item is the live gateway `/compact` + `/stop` **transport round-trip**, which needs a running gateway with an LLM-backed compaction slow enough to interrupt mid-flight — not reproducible in a contributor environment (no model workload). The transport layer above the proven code is a thin `command → runtime.abortEmbeddedAgentRun(sessionId)` call. Two maintainer-owned ways forward:

1. **Accept the source-level chokepoint proof** — ClawSweeper Maintainer option 2. Everything below the transport call is covered end-to-end above, including the negative control.
2. **Capture the live leg via Mantis** (maintainer-only) by posting:

   ```
   @openclaw-mantis telegram live proof: verify a real manual /compact in progress is stopped by /stop, with redacted gateway logs showing compaction abort and active-run cleanup.
   ```

Landing context: #66535 is still open and unfixed; the prior attempts #66569 and #66623 are closed / superseded; there is no competing viable candidate, so this is the live landing path for the canonical bug.

target.title: fix(skills): never wipe target skills dir on sync to prevent data los…
target.body:
…s on partial load

`syncSkillsToWorkspace` used to call `fsp.rm(targetSkillsDir)` before recopying every skill. Any transient failure inside `loadWorkspaceSkillEntries` (a filesystem hiccup, a config parse error, an agent-filter edge case) returns fewer entries than expected and the wipe step destroys skills that the recopy step cannot restore. Users report their entire skills directory disappearing after a single bad load.

Switch to a non-destructive, per-skill incremental sync:

- Only `mkdir` the target skills dir, never wipe it wholesale.
- Build a manifest of (destination dir, source entry) pairs up front, preserving the existing duplicate-basename disambiguation.
- For each entry compare source vs destination mtime via a small 2-level `getNewestMtime` helper; skip re-copying when the destination is already at least as new.
- When a copy is needed, scope the `rm` to that single destination dir so stale files inside it cannot leak into the new copy, then `cp`.
- Emit a debug summary (`manifest/copied/skipped/failed`) to make post-sync diagnostics possible without reconstructing state.

Target directories that are not part of the source manifest — for example skills the user installed directly inside a sandbox — are left untouched. Sandboxes are ephemeral, so a few leftover directories are cheap; losing real user data is not.

Add four regression tests in the existing sync suite:

- Second sync with an empty source no longer wipes previously synced skills.
- User-created skill directories survive an unrelated source sync.
- Unchanged skills are not re-copied (destination mtime preserved).
- Modified source skills are picked up and re-copied.

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem:
- Why it matters:
- What changed:
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

target.title: fix(infra): wrap provider auth resolution in timeout for status --usage --json
target.body:
## Summary
- Wraps `resolveProviderAuths()` in the existing `withTimeout` helper so auth resolution (OAuth token refresh, plugin auth hooks, secret exec resolvers) cannot hang indefinitely
- Returns empty providers on auth timeout instead of blocking forever, allowing the JSON command to complete

## Problem
`openclaw status --usage --json` hangs when called from a non-TTY subprocess (regression from 2026.4.23 to 2026.4.26). The individual provider usage fetches are bounded by `withTimeout`, but `resolveProviderAuths()` — which iterates sequentially over 7 providers calling plugin auth hooks, OAuth token refresh, and secret ref resolvers — had **no timeout boundary**. In a non-TTY subprocess, any of these can block indefinitely.

## Changes

### `src/infra/provider-usage.load.ts`
- Wrapped the `resolveProviderAuths()` call at line 94 in `withTimeout(…, timeoutMs, [])` using the same timeout budget as the fetch phase
- On timeout, returns `[]` (empty auths) which causes `loadProviderUsageSummary` to return `{ providers: [] }` instead of hanging

### `src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts`
- Added mock for `resolveProviderAuths` to support testing the timeout path
- Added test: "returns empty providers when auth resolution exceeds timeout" — mocks auth resolution as a promise that never settles, verifies the function resolves within the timeout

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: non-TTY `status --usage --json` should complete instead of hanging while provider usage auth is resolved.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `fix/status-json-usage-auth-timeout-74085`, head `9852b6356b6a8369bc4f9e19b71745466d6103c5`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node -e '<script uses child_process.spawnSync("node", ["scripts/run-node.mjs", "status", "--usage", "--json"], { timeout: 45000, encoding: "utf8", non-TTY stdio capture }) and prints only exit status, elapsed time, JSON parse status, top-level keys, and usage summary shape>'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "branch": "fix/status-json-usage-auth-timeout-74085",
  "head": "9852b6356b6a8369bc4f9e19b71745466d6103c5",
  "command": "node scripts/run-node.mjs status --usage --json",
  "nonTty": true,
  "exitStatus": 0,
  "signal": null,
  "timedOut": false,
  "elapsedMs": 6513,
  "stdoutBytes": 2757,
  "stderrBytes": 0,
  "parsedJson": true,
  "topLevelKeys": [
    "agents",
    "channelSummary",
    "gateway",
    "gatewayService",
    "heartbeat",
    "lastHeartbeat",
    "memory",
    "memoryPlugin",
    "nodeService",
    "os",
    "queuedSystemEvents",
    "runtimeVersion",
    "secretDiagnostics",
    "sessions",
    "taskAudit",
    "tasks",
    "update",
    "updateChannel",
    "updateChannelSource",
    "usage"
  ],
  "hasUsage": true,
  "usageKeys": [
    "providers",
    "updatedAt"
  ],
  "usageProviders": 1,
  "stderrPreview": []
}
```

Observed result after fix: The non-TTY subprocess exited successfully in about 6.5s, did not hit the 45s timeout, emitted parseable JSON on stdout, and included a top-level `usage` object with provider usage data.

What was not tested: This was a local source checkout proof rather than a packaged install, and it does not print provider names, auth profiles, credentials, or raw usage values.

Fixes #74085

target.title: fix(diagnostic): add hasActiveModelCall to activity snapshot for CLI session recovery
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: `classifySessionAttention` and `isStalledModelCallRecoveryEligible` require `hasActiveEmbeddedRun === true`, which excludes CLI harness sessions (e.g. Codex-backed providers) from stalled-model-call classification and active-abort recovery. The `activeModelCalls` tracking map already captures model calls for ALL session types, but the bool was never exposed to the classification and recovery layers.

**Solution**: Add `hasActiveModelCall` to `DiagnosticSessionActivitySnapshot`, populated from the existing `activeModelCalls.size > 0` tracking. Use it in `classifySessionAttention` for model_call classification and in `isStalledModelCallRecoveryEligible` for recovery eligibility. **No threshold, warn-multiplier, or policy defaults are changed.** Maintainers may adjust abort defaults in a separate change.

**What changed** (5 files, +23/-10):
- `src/logging/diagnostic-run-activity.ts`: +5 -- `hasActiveModelCall` in type + snapshot population
- `src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.ts`: +1/-1 -- use `hasActiveModelCall` for model_call classification
- `src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts`: +11/-9 -- update 4 test cases
- `src/logging/diagnostic.ts`: +1/-1 -- use `hasActiveModelCall` for recovery eligibility
- `src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts`: +7/-4 -- verify CLI model calls are now recovered

**What did NOT change**: `MIN_STALLED_EMBEDDED_RUN_ABORT_MS` (stays 5 min), `STALLED_EMBEDDED_RUN_ABORT_WARN_MULTIPLIER` (stays 3x), all other diagnostic policy.

Ref: #94650

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: CLI harness sessions with hung model calls were detected but never classified as model_call stalls and never recovered -- only embedded agent runs were eligible.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-94650-gateway-watchdog-stall-recovery` at `980509c441`
- **Test runner**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs`

**Root cause**: The `activeModelCalls` map already tracks model calls via `recordModelStarted`/`recordModelStopped` for all session types. But the snapshot type only exposed `hasActiveEmbeddedRun`, not a model-call-specific flag. Classification and recovery both used `hasActiveEmbeddedRun` as the gate for model_call -- semantically wrong. The data was there, just not wired to consumers.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts`

**After-fix evidence**:
```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.src.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  72 passed (72)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 21.28s
```

Updated test: "recovers stalled model calls in non-embedded sessions (e.g. CLI harness)". A CLI session with only a model call now correctly triggers `session.stalled` classification and calls `recoverStuckSession`.

**Observed result after the fix**: The `hasActiveModelCall` flag surfaces existing `activeModelCalls` tracking to classification and recovery layers. CLI harness sessions with hung model calls are now eligible for the same stalled-model-call recovery as embedded agent runs. No abort thresholds were changed. This is a pure correctness fix: model_call classification should gate on model call activity, not embedded-run activity.

**What was not tested**: Live hung Codex provider requiring real active-abort recovery. The fix is at the data-model layer -- it wires existing tracking data to consumers. The recovery mechanism itself is unchanged.

**Evidence provenance**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` on the PR head at Node 24.13 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-25.

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | 72/72 passed | diagnostic.test.ts + diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts |
| Diff | 5 files +23/-10 | API-only, no threshold changes |

## Design decisions (addressing review feedback)

**Why `hasActiveModelCall` instead of modifying `hasActiveEmbeddedRun`?**

`hasActiveEmbeddedRun` tracks whether the session has an active embedded agent run -- a specific lifecycle concept. Model calls are a different activity: they can happen in embedded runs, CLI harness sessions, and webchat sessions. Using `hasActiveEmbeddedRun` as the gate for model_call classification was semantically incorrect. The new flag separates these concerns, derived from the existing `activeModelCalls` tracking that already covers all session types.

**Why not lower abort thresholds in the same PR?**

Threshold policy is a separate product decision from classification correctness. Keeping them separate allows maintainers to review the API fix independently. The existing 5-minute default and 3x warn multiplier are unchanged.

**Why `Ref: #94650` instead of `Fixes`?**

Issue #94650 covers broader scope including restart replay and message deduplication. This PR addresses the model-call recovery eligibility gap.

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area?** `hasActiveModelCall` is derived from `activeModelCalls.size > 0` -- if a model call is started but never stopped (hung provider), the flag stays true. This is correct: hung providers should be classified and recovered.

**Risk level**: Low risk -- pure API fix. The underlying `activeModelCalls` tracking is unchanged. No thresholds, policy defaults, or recovery mechanisms were modified.

**Mitigation**: All 72 diagnostic tests pass. The renamed test explicitly verifies CLI model call recovery. The `hasActiveEmbeddedRun` flag is preserved for embedded-run-specific checks.

**Merge risk declaration**: `session-state` + `availability` -- extends diagnostic recovery eligibility to CLI sessions. This is a bug fix: the heartbeat already detected these stalls, it just could not classify or recover them.

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested. This revision follows the "modify api" approach: pure API/data-model fix without threshold or policy changes.

target.title: fix(control-ui): share gateway token scope across loopback hosts
target.body:
Repair the existing #43254 branch rather than replacing it. This keeps gateway tokens in sessionStorage, shares token scope only across true same-machine loopback aliases, and preserves protocol, port, and pathname boundaries so distinct local gateways remain isolated.

This repair addresses the hydrated bot findings by avoiding broad 127.* hostname matching, removing misleading unreachable IPv6 handling, covering localhost/127/[::1] same-port sharing, and adding negative coverage for DNS hostnames and different ports.

Credit: original implementation and reproduction analysis by @ssdiwu in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/43254. #69647 is related but tracks a different reverse-proxy path persistence bug and should stay separate. Security-routed linked issues remain out of this fix path.

target.title: fix(agents): accept multi-line ANNOUNCE_SKIP on final line
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `isAnnounceSkip()` to also accept multi-line sub-agent output where `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` appears as the final line after a newline, not just as the sole content.
- Add focused unit tests for `sessions-send-tokens.ts` covering exact, whitespace-padded, multi-line, and negative cases.
- Add an e2e regression test for the multi-line announce-skip scenario.

Closes #74071

## Problem

Sub-agents that produce a DM summary block followed by `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` on the final line had their entire summary leak into the parent session as an announcement. The strict equality check `(text ?? "").trim() === ANNOUNCE_SKIP_TOKEN` only matched when the model emitted *just* the token, but real sub-agent outputs typically end with the token after a summary block.

## Fix

`isAnnounceSkip()` now checks two conditions:
1. **Exact match** (existing): trimmed text equals the token
2. **Final-line match** (new): trimmed text ends with the token on its own line (after the last `\n`), with the final line also trimmed to handle whitespace

This preserves all existing behavior (exact and whitespace-padded) and adds the realistic multi-line case reported in the issue. The reporter had been running this patch locally since 2026-04-14 with no regressions.

## Test plan

- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tokens.test.ts` — 16 new focused unit tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts` — 9 existing tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts` — 3 existing tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` — 15 existing tests (all pass)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: sub-agent output ending with a final-line `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` should suppress completion announcement delivery, while `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` followed by more text should not be treated as a silent sentinel.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout using fork PR head `6440ea6b4f`, plus reporter production confirmation on issue #74071's canonical sub-agent workflow.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node --import tsx -e '<script imports isAnnounceSkip, checks single-line sentinel, multiline text ending with ANNOUNCE_SKIP, and ANNOUNCE_SKIP followed by more text>'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "branch": "fork/fix/announce-skip-multiline-74071",
  "head": "6440ea6b4f",
  "command": "node --import tsx isAnnounceSkip proof",
  "cases": [
    {
      "name": "single-line sentinel",
      "lineCount": 1,
      "result": true
    },
    {
      "name": "multiline final-line sentinel",
      "lineCount": 3,
      "result": true
    },
    {
      "name": "sentinel not final",
      "lineCount": 2,
      "result": false
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: a final-line `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` suppresses announcement, including after a summary block; a non-final sentinel does not suppress announcement. The reporter also confirmed an equivalent local patch has run for about 5 weeks across about 5,000+ sub-agent completions with zero observed regressions.
What was not tested: This proof exercises the production sentinel parser locally rather than sending a live sub-agent completion through an external channel.

## Notes

- `isReplySkip()` has the same strict-equality pattern but is not changed here since there is no reported issue for it in the A2A ping-pong flow. It could receive the same treatment in a follow-up if needed.
- Docs currently describe exact-token suppression (`docs/tools/subagents.md`); consider aligning docs if final-line token support becomes the intended contract.

target.title: fix(irc): prevent ghost nick collisions on rejoin after network delay
target.body:
### Summary

- When an IRC connection experiences a network delay and rejoins, the nick collision fallback always appends the same suffix `_`, causing a ghost nick collision with the still-connected session.
- This fix adds a monotonically increasing sequence counter to the fallback nick suffix, so each reconnection attempt generates a unique nick (e.g., `bot_`, `bot_2`, `bot_3`).
- Added 5 regression tests covering: sequential uniqueness, special char sanitization, empty/fallback nick, and long nick truncation.

### Linked issue

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/96064

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Repeated IRC reconnections after network delay fail because every fallback nick is identical (`bot_`). The module-level counter produces `bot_`, `bot_2`, `bot_3`, ... across reconnect calls.
- **Real environment tested:** ngircd 26 running locally on port 6667. Two clients connected — the first holds nick `testbot`, the second attempts `testbot` and receives ERR_NICKNAMEINUSE (433). `buildFallbackNick("testbot")` then produces unique sequential fallback nicks.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Started ngircd 26 locally: `ngircd -n -f /tmp/ngircd.conf`
  2. Real IRC proof: `node /tmp/irc-proof.mjs` — full end-to-end verification
  3. Unit tests: `pnpm test extensions/irc/src/client.test.ts` — 9 passed
  4. Full IRC suite: `pnpm test extensions/irc/` — 15/16 files passed (4 pre-existing locale failures in setup.test.ts unrelated to this change)
  5. Build: `pnpm build` — clean
- **Evidence after fix (real ngircd server):**
  ```
  Phase 1 — Establish ghost session holding nick 'testbot'
    ✅ Ghost connected with nick "testbot"

  Phase 2 — Second client tries nick 'testbot' (simulates rejoin after network delay)
    ✅ Got ERR_NICKNAMEINUSE (433): ":irc.test.local 433 * testbot :Nickname already in use"
    ✅ Ghost nick collision reproduced on real IRC server

  Phase 3 — Fallback nick counter prevents duplicate nicks
    call 1: buildFallbackNick("testbot") → "testbot_"
    call 2: buildFallbackNick("testbot") → "testbot_2"
    call 3: buildFallbackNick("testbot") → "testbot_3"
    call 4: buildFallbackNick("testbot") → "testbot_4"
    call 5: buildFallbackNick("testbot") → "testbot_5"
    ✅ All 5 nicks unique: testbot_, testbot_2, testbot_3, testbot_4, testbot_5

  Phase 4 — Connect to IRC server with each fallback nick
    ✅ "testbot_" → connected successfully
    ✅ "testbot_2" → connected successfully
    ✅ "testbot_3" → connected successfully

  Phase 5 — Edge case coverage
    ✅ sanitize whitespace + special chars: "my bot!" → "mybot_"
    ✅ all-special chars → openclaw fallback: "!!!" → "openclaw_2"
    ✅ empty string → openclaw fallback: "" → "openclaw_3"
    ✅ truncate 50-char nick to max 30 → "aaaa..._4" (≤30 chars)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** `buildFallbackNick("bot")` returns `bot_`, `bot_2`, `bot_3`, ... on successive calls. All nicks are unique and within the 30-char IRC limit. Each nick successfully connects to a real ngircd server. The counter prevents the ghost nick collision loop that previously caused reconnection failures.
- **What was not tested:** Production IRC network with actual NickServ GHOST commands (requires registered nicks and NickServ password).

### Tests and validation

```
pnpm build                                            → clean
pnpm test extensions/irc/src/client.test.ts            → 9 passed (4 existing + 5 new)
pnpm test extensions/irc/                              → 15/16 passed, 65/69 tests
  (4 pre-existing failures in setup.test.ts due to locale, unrelated to this PR)
node /tmp/irc-proof.mjs (real ngircd server)           → ALL CHECKS PASSED
```

### Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change: No (fixes a connection reliability bug)
- Config/default surface change: No
- Security/audit impact: No
- New dependency: No
- Migration needed: No

---
